channel.title: NPR Topics: Religion
channel.description: NPR's stories on U.S. and world religion, spirituality, ethics, and moral issues affecting society and culture. Subscribe to NPR Religion RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from a Syrian church, infused with a mix of sweet fruit and diverse faiths
item.description: Syria's Mar Musa monastery provides the tastiest mulberries, and its services draw from Eastern Orthodox Christian and Sufi customs.
item.content:encoded: Syria's Mar Musa monastery provides the tastiest mulberries, and its services draw from Eastern Orthodox Christian and Sufi customs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Pastor Doug Wilson on why he believes the U.S. should be a Christian theocracy
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: What it means to be excommunicated from the Catholic Church for starting a schism
item.description: The Society of St. Pius X has responded defiantly to Pope Leo XIV's excommunication of the neotraditionalist group. What does the excommunication mean — and how will the schism affect the church?
item.content:encoded: The Society of St. Pius X has responded defiantly to Pope Leo XIV's excommunication of the neotraditionalist group. What does the excommunication mean — and how will the schism affect the church?
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
item.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.content:encoded:
A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Christian missionaries have found a new (virtual) mission territory
item.description: The room may be virtual, but the prayer — and the evangelism — are not.
item.content:encoded:
The room may be virtual, but the prayer — and the evangelism — are not.

(Image credit: Via Geoffery Powell)
item.dc:creator: Fiona Murphy

item.title: What to know about the Society of St. Pius X, the schismatic group excommunicated by the pope
item.description: The Society of St. Pius X was excommunicated by Pope Leo for ordaining bishops without his permission. Its issues with the church run far deeper.
item.content:encoded:
The Society of St. Pius X was excommunicated by Pope Leo for ordaining bishops without his permission. Its issues with the church run far deeper.

(Image credit: Baz Ratner)
item.dc:creator: Noah LaBelle

item.title: Vatican declares Society of St. Pius X in schism, excommunicates bishops
item.description: The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.
item.content:encoded:
The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests.

(Image credit: Baz Ratner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Vatican declares the Society of St. Pius X is in schism, excommunicating its bishops
item.description: On Thursday, the Vatican formalized the excommunications and declared that the Society of St. Pius X had entered schism, breaking communion with the pope and the Catholic Church.
item.content:encoded: On Thursday, the Vatican formalized the excommunications and declared that the Society of St. Pius X had entered schism, breaking communion with the pope and the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: Religious leaders have been talking about aliens for much longer than you think
item.description: God and aliens are having a moment. But what do Christianity and other faiths really teach about ETs?
item.content:encoded: God and aliens are having a moment. But what do Christianity and other faiths really teach about ETs?
item.dc:creator: Jack Jenkins

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
